THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Parr 1—~Sxc. 3(4)} MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (Railway Board) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 10th Februaty, 1999 G.S.R, 96(ft).—In exercise. of the powers conferred by clause (b) of sub-section (2) of Section 30, read with Section 30A of the Railway Claims Tribunal Act, 1987 (54 0f 1987), the Central Governntent hereby makes the following tules further to amend the Railway Claims Tribunal (Salaries and allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice- Chairman and Members) Rules 1989 namely -—~ 1. These rules may be called the Railway Claims Tribunal (Salaries and Allowances and Conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice-Chairman and Members) Amendment Rules, 1999, 2. In the Railway Claims Tribunal (Salaries and Allowances and conditions of Service of Chairman, Vice- Chairman: and Members) Rules, 1989, for tule 3, the following shall be substituted, namely, " - 3. Pay-with effect from the st day of January, 1996 The Chairman shall receive a pay of rupees twenty-six thousand per mensem, a Vice-Chairman shall receive pay in the scale of rmpees 22,400-525-24,500 per mensem and shall in addition, be eligible for a special pay of rupees five hundred per mensem subject to the condition that the pay, together ” with special pay, dogs not exceed rapees twenty six thousand per mensem, a mettber shall receive. pay in the scale of rupees 22,400-525-24,500 per mensem and with effect from the 26th day of September, 1996, the Chairman shall receive a pay of rupees thirty thousand per mensem, a Vice-Chairman, shall receive a pay of rupees twenty-six thousand per mensem, a member shall receive pay in the scale of rupees 22,400-600-26,000 per mensem : : Provided that in the case of appointment as Chairman, Vice-Chairman.or Member of a person who has retired ag a Judge of a High Court, or who has retired from service under the Central Government or a State Government and who is inreceipt of, or has received or has become entitled to, receive any retirement benefits by way of pension, gratuity, employer’s contribution to a Contributory Provident Fund or other forms of retirement benefits, the pay shall be reduced by the gross amount of pension or pension equivalent of employer’s contribution to the Contributory Provident Fund or any other form of retirement benefits, if any, drawn, or to be drawn by him.” EXPLANATORY MEMORANDUM By giving this retrospective effect nobady’s interest will be adversely affected. [No. 94/TCORCTVL-1] PADMAKSHI RAHEIJA, Executive Director, Public Grievances, Railway Board Footnote :—The Principal rules were published vide notification number G.$.R. 844(6), dated the 19th September, 1989, and subsequently amended vide G.S.R. 226(8), dated the 6th December, 1991, G.S.R. 185 (8), dated the Ith April, 1986; G.S.R. 436), dated the 26th September, 1996 and G.S.R. 563 (©), dated the 7th September, 1998.
